summary: Full E2E testing of Asta Creative app. Backend (8/8 pytest pass) - all endpoints working including real LLM (Claude Sonnet via emergentintegrations). Frontend - all pages render, navigation works, contact form submits with sonner toast, Hero 3D scene visible, all data-testid present. CRITICAL UX BUG: 'Made with Emergent' badge overlaps the chat FAB on desktop, blocking real-user clicks (chat only opens via JS dispatch). Chat backend itself works perfectly and returns coherent Bahasa Indonesia replies.

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 200 instead of 201 on creation; acceptable but not strictly REST-idiomatic

Catches all LLM exceptions and returns 200 with fallback text. Hides real LLM failures from monitoring. Consider returning 502 plus persisting flag, or at least logging severity. Today's run did reach real Claude Sonnet successfully.

ui_bugs.issue: The Emergent platform badge (#emergent-badge anchor, fixed bottom-right) overlaps the chat-fab button. elementsFromPoint at the FAB location returns the emergent badge as topmost element, intercepting clicks. Even Playwright force=True click does not open the chat window; only a direct JS .click() works. Real users on desktop 1920x1080 will likely be unable to click the FAB.

issues: Chat FAB (bottom-6 right-6, z-55) collides with platform's bottom-right 'Made with Emergent' badge. Move FAB to bottom-24 (above badge) or right-28, or raise z-index above the badge to fix UX.

action_items: Move ChatWidget FAB above the Emergent badge: change `fixed bottom-6 right-6` to `fixed bottom-24 right-6` (or `bottom-6 right-28`) and/or raise z-index so it sits above #emergent-badge. Without this fix, end users cannot open the CS chat on desktop.
Optional: have /api/chat return a proper 502 (or include an `error: true` field) when the LLM call fails so observability isn't lost; currently the fallback is silently returned with 200.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: contact records persist `created_at` as ISO string but ContactRecord.created_at is typed as str - consistent but consider datetime for type hints.
ChatWidget.jsx: localStorage session id is fine but consider truncating very long histories before resending (server already loads 50 docs but only logs them, doesn't pass to LLM as context).

Backend's /api/chat handler loads `history_docs` from Mongo but never passes them to LlmChat - relying solely on LlmChat session memory. If LlmChat is stateless per request, multi-turn coherence may degrade across restarts.
No rate limiting on /api/chat - public endpoint with paid LLM key could be abused.
test_credentials: Not required - no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test contact rows and 1 chat session with TEST_ prefix for verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the FAB overlap (move chat FAB above emergent-badge), re-run the playwright chat test: click [data-testid=chat-fab] normally (no force), expect [data-testid=chat-window] to appear, then chat-input + chat-send should produce assistant reply within ~15s. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the regression suite (8 tests, all passing as of 2026-05-31).
rca_of_the_issue: Chat FAB unclickable RCA: The Emergent platform automatically injects an anchor tag with id='emergent-badge' fixed at the bottom-right of every preview deployment. Inspecting elementsFromPoint(1872, 1032) on home page returned [A#emergent-badge, svg, BUTTON.chat-fab,...] - badge is topmost stacking context, capturing pointer events. Mitigation: bump FAB z-index higher (e.g., z-[999]) AND/OR offset the FAB position vertically (bottom-24) so it no longer overlaps the badge geometry. Without offset, even higher z-index alone may still visually overlap the badge.
